canonical_definition: The inherent tendency of a large language model to favor certain outputs, such as specific conversational strategies, over other equally or more appropriate alternatives.
papers: Reward-Shifted Speculative Sampling Is An Efficient Test-Time Weak-to-Strong Aligner

definitions.text: The inherent tendency of a large language model to favor certain outputs, such as specific conversational strategies, over other equally or more appropriate alternatives.
The tendency of a model to over-prioritize human input regardless of its correctness, stemming from reinforcement learning that aligns with potentially biased human preferences.

relationships.paper: Enhancing Multiple Dimensions of Trustworthiness in LLMs via Sparse Activation Control
